feedback.text: Today, some large video game publishers allow themselves to end the life of their video games without offering a solution for the end of life of their products for players, who, let us recall, are customers of those publishers who have paid for using those products. For example, The Crew by Ubisoft is no longer playable today (the game cannot even be launched...), even for players with a physical version of the video game. Anthem by Electronic Arts will suffer the same fate in January 2026. The Digital Fairness Act is intended to prevent unfair commercial practices related to dark patterns, if I understand what that means, that kind of practices by video game publishers seems to me to correspond to that issue, or in any event is close enough to be discussed and debated. A European Citizens’ Initiative is already underway in an attempt to combat such practices, called Stop Destroying Videogames and has already won more than one million four hundred thousand signatures, showing the public’s interest in this phenomenon. I hope my opinion will shed some light on the issue. Thank you.
